In honor of Navy Lieutenant Michael Murphy, 29, of Patchogue, NY, who was killed in Afghanistan June 28th, 2005.

sections.title: 40-60 Minute Machine Flush / MOB
lines: Rotate through as many modalities as you can, excluding running but not walking. Pace should feel very very easy, but elicit a nice sweat to aid in your mobility routine.

sections.title: Before completing, ask yourself, do you still feel Murph?? If  so, skip this or keep it very light!
lines: Lower the bar down to mid thigh with the torso getting out over the bar slightly, so you have to mimic getting back to power position as you would from the floor.

sections.title: 12:00 Zone 2 Cool Down
lines: Your Zone 2 today should stay between 160bpm minus your age and 180bpm minus your age.

sections.title: Warm up:
lines: Build from resting heartrate to 180bpm minus your age slowly over 12:00. This will feel incredibly slow, but will help your session a lot. Feel free to mix up machines, but end on rower.

sections.title: Cool down:
lines: Spend 4:00 on the rower, keeping your heartrate above 150bpm minus your age, then slowly work down to resting for the next 8:00 on any machine or walking.

sections.title: WORKOUT
lines: Most athletes chase the clock, the load, and the score, and quietly skip the one thing that's most likely to break them through their next plateau. Deliberate practice isn't a rest day in disguise; it's how you unlock the fitness you already have the capacity for but haven't been able to access yet.
